activities_projects.title: Proto-Industrialisation Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the decentralized production system prior to the factory age
steps: Research the link between town merchants and rural craftspeople
Create a complex flowchart showing the wool production chain: Stapler -> Spinner -> Weaver -> Fuller -> Dyer
Identify London’s role as the 'finishing centre' in this international network
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between domestic production and the modern factory system
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the production sequence and logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Steam Engine Paradox Debate
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the slow adoption of technology in 19th-century Britain
steps: Form teams representing Factory Owners vs. Inventors (James Watt/Matthew Boulton)
Debate why owners preferred manual labor over machines (high repair costs, seasonal demand, abundance of cheap labor)
Write a summary on the socio-economic factors that delayed the 'Age of Steam'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critical understanding of the relationship between labor supply and technological shift

activities_projects.title: Gomastha vs. Weaver Conflict Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine the impact of the East India Company's direct control over Indian textiles
materials: Primary source excerpts on weaver petitions
steps: Research the role of the 'Gomastha' (paid servant) versus the traditional merchant
Analyze why the system of advances (loans) led to the debt bondage of weavers
Map the migration of weavers fleeing to other villages to escape Company control
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Insights into the dismantling of traditional Indian handicraft autonomy
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical depth and analytical clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Jobber' Recruitment Audit
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the informal power structures in early Indian industrialization
steps: Identify the 'Jobber' as a figure of authority and his role in rural-urban migration
Analyze how the Jobber exploited his position for personal gain (gifts/money)
Compare this informal recruitment with modern HR systems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the reliance on social ties for labor mobilization in colonies
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of socio-political power dynamics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Marketing the Empire: Label Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the use of iconography in colonial advertisements
materials: Prints of Manchester cloth labels (featuring Gods/Royalty)
steps: Analyze why British manufacturers used images of Krishna, Saraswati, or King George on cloth bundles
Explain how these images made foreign goods feel familiar to Indian consumers
Design an original label for a 19th-century product using 'local' imagery to increase sales
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesis of psychology, marketing, and cultural imperialism
activities_projects.evaluation: Semiotic analysis and creative execution (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Technological Displacement Storyboard
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the resistance of workers to new machinery
steps: Research the attacks on the Spinning Jenny by women in the woollen industry
Draw a 6-panel storyboard depicting: Labor fear -> Introduction of machine -> Conflict -> Social impact
Caption each panel with the economic rationale behind the workers' hostility
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the concept of technological unemployment and social friction
activities_projects.evaluation: Narrative flow and historical accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Small-Scale Persistence Map
activities_projects.objective: Understand why large factories did not completely eliminate handlooms in India
steps: Identify centers like Salem, Madurai, and Banaras that specialized in intricate weaves
Plot the growth of fly-shuttle technology in the early 20th century
Write an essay on why 'coarse cloth' buyers switched to mills while 'fine cloth' buyers remained loyal to weavers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognition of the resilience of specialized cottage industries
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of mapping and economic reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Industrial War: WWI Economic Audit
activities_projects.objective: Trace the shift in trade dominance during the Great War
steps: Trace how British mills were diverted to war production, leaving the Indian market open
Analyze the boom in Indian mills (jute bags, uniforms) during the war years
Explain why Manchester could never recapture its market position in India after 1918
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects global geopolitical conflict to regional industrial growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Synthesis of war impact on global trade patterns (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Urbanization and the 'Outsider' Perspective
activities_projects.objective: Examine the housing and living conditions of the industrial poor
materials: Excerpts from 'London Labour and the London Poor'
steps: Analyze the divide between the 'Respectable' classes and the 'Vagrants' in industrial London
Create a comparative report on the 'Chawls' of Mumbai and the 'Tenements' of Manchester
Discuss the environmental impact of early industrial clusters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of the social costs of unplanned industrial urbanization
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparative sociological research (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historiography of Industrialization
activities_projects.objective: Challenge the 'Grand Narrative' of a sudden industrial revolution
steps: Critically evaluate the term 'Industrial Revolution'—was it a revolution or an evolution?
Compare the 'Age of Machinery' with the 'Handmade Era' in terms of social dignity of the worker
Conclude with a thesis on how the 'Global' world was built on the backs of both the machine and the hand
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Advanced understanding of historical interpretation and continuity
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and philosophical synthesis (10 marks)
